properties.displayName: Akamai Security Events (via Codeless Connector Framework)

connectorUiConfig.title: Akamai Security Events (via Codeless Connector Framework)
connectorUiConfig.descriptionMarkdown: The [Akamai SIEM Connector](https://techdocs.akamai.com/siem-integration/docs) ingests web application firewall (WAF) security events from the [Akamai SIEM Integration API](https://techdocs.akamai.com/siem-integration/reference/get-config) into Microsoft Sentinel. The connector collects detailed security event data including attack information, geographic context, and HTTP request/response metadata. Authentication uses Akamai EdgeGrid - provide your three EdgeGrid credentials (client_token, access_token, client_secret) plus the API hostname from your .edgerc file. The platform runtime computes a fresh HMAC-SHA-256 signed Authorization header on every poll request per the EdgeGrid specification.

sampleQueries.query:
AkamaiSIEMEvent
 | sort by TimeGenerated desc
 | take 50

AkamaiSIEMEvent
 | summarize Count = count() by AppliedAction
 | sort by Count desc

AkamaiSIEMEvent
 | summarize Count = count() by ClientIp
 | top 10 by Count desc

AkamaiSIEMEvent
 | summarize Count = count() by GeoCountry
 | sort by Count desc

sampleQueries.description: Denied requests in the last 24 hours
sampleQueries.query:
AkamaiSIEMEvent
 | where TimeGenerated > ago(24h)
 | where AppliedAction == 'deny'
 | project TimeGenerated, ClientIp, HttpPath, HttpMethod, PolicyId, GeoCountry
 | sort by TimeGenerated desc

dataTypes.name: AkamaiSIEMEvent
dataTypes.lastDataReceivedQuery:
AkamaiSIEMEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(12h)
| summarize Time = max(TimeGenerated)
| where isnotempty(Time)

resourceProvider.permissionsDisplayText: Read and Write permissions are required.

customs.name: Akamai EdgeGrid API Credentials
customs.description: **Client Token**, **Access Token**, and **Client Secret** are required for EdgeGrid HMAC-SHA-256 authentication. These credentials are generated from the [Akamai Control Center](https://control.akamai.com/) under **Identity & Access** > **API Clients**.

instructionSteps.title: Step 1 - Enable Data Collection for SIEM in Akamai Security Center
instructionSteps.description: Turn on SIEM integration in your Akamai security configuration and note the Web Security Configuration ID.

parameters.content:
1. Log in to [Akamai Security Center](https://control.akamai.com/apps/security-center/).
2. Select your security configuration and open the **SIEM Integration** section.
3. Set **Data collection for SIEM** to **On**.
4. Under **Turn on SIEM integration for**, select the security policies to monitor (or choose **All Security Policies**).
5. Choose a **SIEM Event Version** (SIEM Version 01 is recommended).
6. Copy the **Web Security Configuration ID** shown on the page - you will need it in Step 3 below.

instructionSteps.title: Step 2 - Provision EdgeGrid API credentials
instructionSteps.description: Create an Akamai EdgeGrid API client with the Manage SIEM role and download the .edgerc credentials file.

parameters.content:
1. In [Akamai Control Center](https://control.akamai.com/apps/identity-management/#/tabs/users/list), go to **Identity & Access** > **Users**.
2. Create a new user (or select an existing service account) and assign the **Manage SIEM** role.
3. Go to **Identity & Access** > **API Clients** and click **Create API client** > **Quick**.
4. Select the **SIEM** API service and grant **READ** access.
5. Click **Create** and then **Download** to save the `.edgerc` file.
6. The `.edgerc` file contains the four values you will enter in Step 3:

```
[default]
client_secret = <your-client-secret>
host = <your-host>.luna.akamaiapis.net
access_token = akab-<your-access-token>
client_token = akab-<your-client-token>
```

The connector platform computes a fresh HMAC-SHA-256 signed Authorization header on every poll - you do not need to sign anything manually.

instructionSteps.title: Step 3 - Connect Akamai SIEM to Microsoft Sentinel

parameters.content: **Multi-tenant support:** This connector supports ingesting security events from multiple Akamai security configurations in parallel. Click **Add Connection** once per configuration, supplying that configuration's EdgeGrid credentials, hostname, and Config ID. Each connection is tracked and managed independently in the grid below.

parameters.label: Add Connection
parameters.title: Configure Akamai SIEM API Connection
parameters.subtitle: Connect to an Akamai security configuration to ingest WAF events

dataFlows.transformKql: source | extend TimeGenerated = iff(isempty(tostring(httpMessage.start)) or isnull(tolong(httpMessage.start)), now(), datetime(1970-01-01) + tolong(httpMessage.start) * 1s) , EventType = ['type'] , ApiVersion = ['version'] , Format = ['format'] , ConfigId = tostring(attackData.configId) , PolicyId = tostring(attackData.policyId) , ClientIp = tostring(attackData.clientIP) , AppliedAction = tostring(attackData.appliedAction) , ClientReputation = tostring(attackData.clientReputation) , ApiId = tostring(attackData.apiId) , SlowPostAction = tostring(attackData.slowPostAction) , SlowPostRate = tostring(attackData.slowPostRate) , RuleActions = tostring(attackData.ruleActions) , RuleData = tostring(attackData.ruleData) , RuleMessages = tostring(attackData.ruleMessages) , RuleSelectors = tostring(attackData.ruleSelectors) , RuleTags = tostring(attackData.ruleTags) , RuleVersions = tostring(attackData.ruleVersions) , Rules = tostring(attackData.rules) , GeoAsn = tostring(geo.asn) , GeoCity = tostring(geo.city) , GeoContinent = tostring(geo.continent) , GeoCountry = tostring(geo.country) , GeoRegionCode = tostring(geo.regionCode) , HttpRequestId = tostring(httpMessage.requestId) , HttpRequestTime = iff(isnull(httpMessage) or isempty(tostring(httpMessage.start)), datetime(null), datetime(1970-01-01) + (tolong(httpMessage.start) * 1s)) , HttpHost = tostring(httpMessage.host) , HttpMethod = tostring(httpMessage.method) , HttpPath = tostring(httpMessage.path) , HttpPort = tostring(httpMessage.port) , HttpProtocol = tostring(httpMessage.protocol) , HttpQueryString = tostring(httpMessage.query) , HttpResponseCode = tostring(httpMessage.status) , HttpResponseBytes = tostring(httpMessage.bytes) , HttpRequestHeaders = tostring(httpMessage.requestHeaders) , HttpResponseHeaders = tostring(httpMessage.responseHeaders) , BotScore = tostring(botData.botScore) , BotResponseSegment = tostring(botData.responseSegment) , ClientAppBundleId = tostring(clientData.appBundleId) , ClientAppVersion = tostring(clientData.appVersion) , ClientSdkVersion = tostring(clientData.sdkVersion) , ClientTelemetryType = tostring(clientData.telemetryType) , IdentityJa4 = tostring(identity.ja4) , IdentityTlsFingerprintV2 = tostring(identity.tlsFingerprintV2) , IdentityTlsFingerprintV3 = tostring(identity.tlsFingerprintV3) , UserRiskAllow = tostring(userRiskData.allow) , UserRiskEmailDomain = tostring(userRiskData.emailDomain) , UserRiskGeneral = tostring(userRiskData.general) , UserRiskOriginUserId = tostring(userRiskData.originUserId) , UserRiskRisk = tostring(userRiskData.risk) , UserRiskScore = tostring(userRiskData.score) , UserRiskStatus = tostring(userRiskData.status) , UserRiskTrust = tostring(userRiskData.trust) , UserRiskUsername = tostring(userRiskData.username) , UserRiskUuid = tostring(userRiskData.uuid) | where isnotempty(EventType) | project TimeGenerated , EventType , ApiVersion , Format , ConfigId , PolicyId , ClientIp , AppliedAction , ClientReputation , ApiId , SlowPostAction , SlowPostRate , RuleActions , RuleData , RuleMessages , RuleSelectors , RuleTags , RuleVersions , Rules , GeoAsn , GeoCity , GeoContinent , GeoCountry , GeoRegionCode , HttpRequestId , HttpRequestTime , HttpHost , HttpMethod , HttpPath , HttpPort , HttpProtocol , HttpQueryString , HttpResponseCode , HttpResponseBytes , HttpRequestHeaders , HttpResponseHeaders , BotScore , BotResponseSegment , ClientAppBundleId , ClientAppVersion , ClientSdkVersion , ClientTelemetryType , IdentityJa4 , IdentityTlsFingerprintV2 , IdentityTlsFingerprintV3 , UserRiskAllow , UserRiskEmailDomain , UserRiskGeneral , UserRiskOriginUserId , UserRiskRisk , UserRiskScore , UserRiskStatus , UserRiskTrust , UserRiskUsername , UserRiskUuid

schema.name: AkamaiSIEMEvent_CL
schema.description: Akamai WAF security events ingested from the Akamai SIEM Integration API. Each row represents a single security event and includes attack details, geographic context, and the associated HTTP request/response metadata.

columns.name: EventType
columns.description: Event type identifier, typically 'akamai_siem'.

columns.name: ApiVersion
columns.description: API schema version for this event.

columns.name: Format
columns.description: Response format descriptor returned by the API (e.g., 'json').

columns.name: ConfigId
columns.description: Unique identifier of the Akamai security configuration that generated the event.

columns.name: PolicyId
columns.description: Security policy identifier within the configuration.

columns.name: ClientIp
columns.description: IP address of the client that triggered the security event.

columns.name: AppliedAction
columns.description: Action taken in response to the attack (e.g., alert, deny, abort).

columns.name: ClientReputation
columns.description: Client reputation score or category as assessed by Akamai.

columns.name: ApiId
columns.description: API definition identifier if the event is associated with an API endpoint.

columns.name: SlowPostAction
columns.description: Slow POST protection action applied (e.g., alert, abort).

columns.name: SlowPostRate
columns.description: Observed rate for a slow POST attack attempt.

columns.name: RuleActions
columns.description: Semicolon-delimited, URL-encoded, base64-encoded list of rule actions that triggered.

columns.name: RuleData
columns.description: Semicolon-delimited, URL-encoded, base64-encoded rule data payload for matched rules.

columns.name: RuleMessages
columns.description: Semicolon-delimited, URL-encoded, base64-encoded human-readable messages for matched rules.

columns.name: RuleSelectors
columns.description: Semicolon-delimited, URL-encoded, base64-encoded selectors (request parts) that matched the rules.

columns.name: RuleTags
columns.description: Semicolon-delimited, URL-encoded, base64-encoded tags classifying the matched rules.

columns.name: RuleVersions
columns.description: Semicolon-delimited, base64-encoded list of rule versions corresponding to each matched rule.

columns.name: Rules
columns.description: Semicolon-delimited, base64-encoded list of matched rule IDs (e.g., '950002;950006').

columns.name: GeoAsn
columns.description: Autonomous System Number (ASN) of the client's network.

columns.name: GeoCity
columns.description: City derived from the client's IP address.

columns.name: GeoContinent
columns.description: Numeric continent identifier derived from the client's IP address, as returned by the Akamai geo lookup.

columns.name: GeoCountry
columns.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code derived from the client's IP address.

columns.name: GeoRegionCode
columns.description: Region or state code derived from the client's IP address.

columns.name: HttpRequestId
columns.description: Unique request identifier assigned by Akamai.

columns.name: HttpRequestTime
columns.description: Unix epoch timestamp (seconds) when the HTTP request was received.

columns.name: HttpHost
columns.description: HTTP Host header value from the inbound request.

columns.name: HttpMethod
columns.description: HTTP method of the request (e.g., GET, POST, PUT).

columns.name: HttpPath
columns.description: Request URL path.

columns.name: HttpPort
columns.description: TCP port number used for the connection.

columns.name: HttpProtocol
columns.description: HTTP protocol version (e.g., HTTP/1.1, HTTP/2).

columns.name: HttpQueryString
columns.description: URL query string of the request.

columns.name: HttpResponseCode
columns.description: HTTP status code returned by the origin or WAF (e.g., 200, 403). Source field: httpMessage.status.

columns.name: HttpResponseBytes
columns.description: Number of bytes in the HTTP response body, returned as a string by the API.

columns.name: HttpRequestHeaders
columns.description: URL-encoded request headers captured by the WAF.

columns.name: HttpResponseHeaders
columns.description: URL-encoded response headers captured by the WAF.

columns.name: BotScore
columns.description: Bot risk score assigned by Akamai Bot Manager (0-100; higher = more bot-like).

columns.name: BotResponseSegment
columns.description: Bot Manager response segment identifier used to categorise the bot action taken.

columns.name: ClientAppBundleId
columns.description: Mobile application bundle identifier, present for requests from Akamai SDK-instrumented mobile apps.

columns.name: ClientAppVersion
columns.description: Version of the mobile application, present for SDK-instrumented requests.

columns.name: ClientSdkVersion
columns.description: Version of the Akamai mobile SDK embedded in the client application.

columns.name: ClientTelemetryType
columns.description: Telemetry source type code indicating how the client data was collected.

columns.name: IdentityJa4
columns.description: JA4 TLS client fingerprint - a compact string representing the TLS handshake characteristics of the client.

columns.name: IdentityTlsFingerprintV2
columns.description: Akamai TLS fingerprint v2 (hex string) derived from the client TLS handshake.

columns.name: IdentityTlsFingerprintV3
columns.description: Akamai TLS fingerprint v3 string; includes version prefix and fingerprint value.

columns.name: UserRiskAllow
columns.description: Indicates whether the user was allowed (1) or blocked (0) based on user risk evaluation.

columns.name: UserRiskEmailDomain
columns.description: Email domain of the user associated with this request.

columns.name: UserRiskGeneral
columns.description: General risk signals as pipe-delimited key:value pairs (e.g., 'duc_1h:10|duc_1d:30').

columns.name: UserRiskOriginUserId
columns.description: Unique user identifier from the origin system (e.g., internal user ID).

columns.name: UserRiskRisk
columns.description: Risk signal breakdown as pipe-delimited key:value pairs (e.g., 'udfp:1325gdg4/M|unp:74256/H').

columns.name: UserRiskScore
columns.description: Composite user risk score (0-100; higher = riskier).

columns.name: UserRiskStatus
columns.description: User risk evaluation status code.

columns.name: UserRiskTrust
columns.description: Trust signal breakdown as pipe-delimited key:value pairs (e.g., 'ugp:US').

columns.name: UserRiskUsername
columns.description: Authenticated username associated with the request.

columns.name: UserRiskUuid
columns.description: UUID of the user risk session, used to correlate risk signals across requests.

connectorUiConfig.title: Akamai Security Events (via Codeless Connector Framework)
connectorUiConfig.descriptionMarkdown: The [Akamai SIEM Connector](https://techdocs.akamai.com/siem-integration/docs) ingests web application firewall (WAF) security events from the [Akamai SIEM Integration API](https://techdocs.akamai.com/siem-integration/reference/get-config) into Microsoft Sentinel. The connector collects detailed security event data including attack information, geographic context, and HTTP request/response metadata. Authentication uses Akamai EdgeGrid - provide your three EdgeGrid credentials (client_token, access_token, client_secret) plus the API hostname from your .edgerc file. The platform runtime computes a fresh HMAC-SHA-256 signed Authorization header on every poll request per the EdgeGrid specification.

AkamaiSIEMEvent
 | sort by TimeGenerated desc
 | take 50

AkamaiSIEMEvent
 | summarize Count = count() by AppliedAction
 | sort by Count desc

AkamaiSIEMEvent
 | summarize Count = count() by ClientIp
 | top 10 by Count desc

AkamaiSIEMEvent
 | summarize Count = count() by GeoCountry
 | sort by Count desc

instructionSteps.title: Step 1 - Enable Data Collection for SIEM in Akamai Security Center
instructionSteps.description: Turn on SIEM integration in your Akamai security configuration and note the Web Security Configuration ID.

parameters.content:
1. Log in to [Akamai Security Center](https://control.akamai.com/apps/security-center/).
2. Select your security configuration and open the **SIEM Integration** section.
3. Set **Data collection for SIEM** to **On**.
4. Under **Turn on SIEM integration for**, select the security policies to monitor (or choose **All Security Policies**).
5. Choose a **SIEM Event Version** (SIEM Version 01 is recommended).
6. Copy the **Web Security Configuration ID** shown on the page - you will need it in Step 3 below.

instructionSteps.title: Step 2 - Provision EdgeGrid API credentials
instructionSteps.description: Create an Akamai EdgeGrid API client with the Manage SIEM role and download the .edgerc credentials file.

parameters.content:
1. In [Akamai Control Center](https://control.akamai.com/apps/identity-management/#/tabs/users/list), go to **Identity & Access** > **Users**.

3. Go to **Identity & Access** > **API Clients** and click **Create API client** > **Quick**.
4. Select the **SIEM** API service and grant **READ** access.
5. Click **Create** and then **Download** to save the `.edgerc` file.
6. The `.edgerc` file contains the four values you will enter in Step 3:

```
[default]
client_secret = <your-client-secret>
host = <your-host>.luna.akamaiapis.net
access_token = akab-<your-access-token>
client_token = akab-<your-client-token>
```

The connector platform computes a fresh HMAC-SHA-256 signed Authorization header on every poll - you do not need to sign anything manually.

properties.displayName: Akamai Security Events (via Codeless Connector Framework)

connectorDefinitionName.defaultValue: Akamai Security Events (via Codeless Connector Framework)

properties.description: parser_AkamaiSIEMEventAliasFunction Data Parser with template version 3.0.1

properties.descriptionHtml:
Note: Please refer to the following before installing the solution:

• Review the solution Release Notes

• There may be known issues pertaining to this Solution, please refer to them before installing.

The Akamai DDOS Protection Solution for Microsoft Sentinel enables you to ingest DDoS protection and web application firewall (WAF) security events from the Akamai SIEM Integration API into Microsoft Sentinel using the Codeless Connector Framework (CCF). This solution provides visibility into attack data, geographic context, and HTTP request/response metadata for security monitoring, threat detection, and investigation.

Underlying Microsoft Technologies used:

This solution takes a dependency on the following technologies, and some of these dependencies either may be in Preview state or might result in additional ingestion or operational costs:

  1. Codeless Connector Framework (CCF)

  2. Log Ingestion API

  3. Data Collection Rules (DCR)

Data Connectors: 1, Parsers: 1

Learn more about Microsoft Sentinel | Learn more about Solutions
